operating_state: Total revenue increased 13% to $26.3 billion driven by net interest income rising $2.9 billion to $14.4 billion from higher interest rates and loan growth, with net interest yield expanding 51 basis points to 2.20% on FTE basis. Global Markets revenue increased from improved FICC trading performance while equities revenue decreased due to weaker client activity. Commercial loans increased primarily from commercial real estate and U.S. commercial growth, while consumer portfolio outstandings declined across residential mortgage, home equity, and credit card segments from seasonal patterns and paydown activity.
cost_structure: Noninterest expense increased to $16.2 billion from continued investments in people and technology and higher FDIC expense, partially offset by lower revenue-related incentive compensation, improving efficiency ratio to 61.84% from 65.95%. Net charge-offs increased to $807 million driven by credit card losses normalizing from historic lows and higher overdraft losses from industry-wide check fraud activity. Provision for credit losses increased to $931 million primarily from consumer portfolio reserve builds due to higher-than-expected credit card balances, partially offset by improved macroeconomic conditions benefiting commercial portfolio.
material_events: Board declared quarterly common dividend of $0.22 per share while repurchasing $2.2 billion of common stock under the Board-authorized program. Bank deposited $5 billion into First Republic Bank as part of industry support package, subsequently becoming JPMorgan Chase depositor following FDIC receivership. Long-term debt issuances totaled $14.3 billion while maturities and redemptions were $11.3 billion, resulting in net debt increase.
macro_exposures: Office commercial real estate exposure represents 26 percent of commercial real estate portfolio with criticized exposure and significant maturities by end of 2024, experiencing collateral value declines though loans remain well-secured with majority Class A properties. Rising interest rates and deposit outflows creating ongoing uncertainty for global markets and financial institutions with potential for continued volatility from additional bank failures. Non-U.S. exposure decreased primarily in developed markets while continued monitoring of industries facing macroeconomic challenges including geopolitical tensions and inflationary pressures.
forward_outlook: Corporation submitted 2023 CCAR capital plan with Federal Reserve results expected by June 30, 2023, while G-SIB surcharge expected to increase to 3.0 percent from 2.5 percent on January 1, 2024. Asset sensitivity to parallel rate increases decreased during the quarter due to deposit product mix changes, though banking book remains asset sensitive with estimated benefit from 100 basis point parallel rate increase.
uncertainty_profile: Episodes of increased economic and market volatility may continue with potential worsening from additional bank failures adversely affecting business operations from unresolved impacts of rising interest rates, inflation, and geopolitical instability. Commercial real estate markets face ongoing disruptions from demand uncertainty and capital market conditions, with future office space demand uncertain as companies evaluate hybrid employment models.
